understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by placing all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked '+' in the semantic grid, with every goal tile occupied simultaneously to trigger WIN, and then progress through all levels until the run reports WIN for the whole game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: On each turn, the currently selected piece (shown as 'P' and identified by player.id/position) could be moved one grid step (3 pixels per cell) using MOVE_* actions; movement could be blocked by walls or other objects, and some adjacent objects were pushable blocks that would slide in the movement direction along with the selected piece when pushed. Levels could contain multiple selectables ('S'), and control could be switched by CLICKing the exact pixel/grid position of another selectable; the movement.*_blocked flags were only hints indicating an adjacent object, not a guarantee the move would fail.
